somewhat.example: Although James shouldn't have lied to Mary, she still felt somewhat guilty about being so angry with him.
somewhat.example_tw: 雖然詹姆斯不該對瑪麗撒謊，但她對他如此生氣仍感到有些內疚。

composition.definition: the materials that something is made up of and how these materials are arranged
composition.example: A substance's chemical composition refers to the chemical elements it contains and their amounts.
composition.example_tw: 物質的化學成分是指其所含的化學元素及其含量。

alter.example: David usually alters his hairstyle once every three months when he gets bored with it.
alter.example_tw: 大衛通常每三個月換一次髮型，因為他厭倦了。

suit.translation: 滿足(某人)需要;合(某人)心意
suit.definition: to benefit or be useful to someone in some way
suit.example: Working from home suits Frank because it enables him to keep a flexible schedule.
suit.example_tw: 在家工作適合法蘭克，因為這能讓他保持彈性的時間表。

scale.definition: the degree or size of something in comparison with similar things
scale.example: KYMCO manufactures scooters and motorbikes on an extremely large scale, producing more than 500,000 vehicles every year.
scale.example_tw: 光陽公司以極大的規模生產速克達和摩托車，每年生產超過 50 萬輛。

comprehensive.definition: including all the necessary facts, details, or problems that may be involved
comprehensive.example: Since the team of researchers interviewed more than five thousand people for their survey, it was the most comprehensive study ever conducted.
comprehensive.example_tw: 由於研究團隊為他們的調查採訪了五千多人，這是迄今為止最全面的研究。

prominent.definition: known by many; of great importance
prominent.example: Kevin played a prominent part in the company since he was one of the original founders.
prominent.example_tw: 凱文在公司裡扮演了重要的角色，因為他是最初的創始人之一。

commerce.definition: the exchange of goods or services for money
commerce.example: The government decided to broaden the scope of the nation's commerce by finding new countries to trade with.
commerce.example_tw: 政府決定藉由尋找新的貿易國家來擴大國家的貿易範圍。

tranquil.example: Jamie had a great time in this tranquil village since she could temporarily leave work behind and relax.
tranquil.example_tw: 潔米在這個平靜的村莊度過了美好的時光，因為她可以暫時拋開工作放鬆一下。

vast.definition: very big in space, size, or amount
vast.example: The runner had a vast amount of energy and ran the marathon in just under three hours.
vast.example_tw: 這名跑者擁有大量的體力，在不到三小時內就跑完了馬拉松。

literally.definition: in a way that expresses the basic or original meaning of a word or phrase
literally.example: The term alter ego, which literally means "other self," can refer to either another personality or a close friend of someone.
literally.example_tw: alter ego 這個詞字面上的意思是「另一個自我」，可以指另一個性格或某人的密友。

tide.definition: the upward and downward movements of the ocean caused by the pull of the moon and sun
tide.example: Peter was pulled into the water by the strong tides when he was surfing. Luckily, he was soon rescued.
tide.example_tw: 彼得衝浪時被強勁的潮汐拉入水中。幸運的是，他很快就獲救了。

volume.example: The boss was deeply worried when he saw that sales volumes of the company had fallen by 30%.
volume.example_tw: 老闆看到公司銷售總量下降了 30% 時深感擔憂。

surge.definition: to suddenly rise or move in a particular direction with great force
surge.example: At the sight of the legendary basketball player, the fans surged forward.
surge.example_tw: 一看到這位傳奇籃球員，球迷們便湧上前去。

likelihood.definition: the possibility that something will occur
likelihood.example: In all likelihood, it will start raining since the sky is already dark and cloudy.
likelihood.example_tw: 天空已經烏雲密布，極有可能會開始下雨。

solid.definition: firm or hard, with a concrete shape
solid.example: The child threw a solid block of wood at the window and broke the glass.
solid.example_tw: 孩子朝窗戶扔了一塊堅硬的木頭，打破了玻璃。

shift.example: The girl felt anxious and shifted uncomfortably from one foot to the other.
shift.example_tw: 女孩感到焦慮，不自在地將重心從一隻腳移到另一腳。

elevation.definition: the height of a place above the sea level
elevation.example: In general, air pressure decreases with increasing elevation.
elevation.example_tw: 一般來說，氣壓會隨著海拔的升高而降低。

sustain.example: The movie was so amusing that it sustained the children's interest until the end.
sustain.example_tw: 這部電影非常有趣，讓孩子們直到最後都保持著興趣。

accelerate.definition: to speed up or to make something happen faster
accelerate.example: Paul accidentally pressed the gas pedal, and the car accelerated quickly.
accelerate.example_tw: 保羅不小心踩到了油門，車子很快就加速了。

contend.definition: to compete against someone or something, usually to obtain a prize or power
contend.example: There are ten teams contending for the championship in this year's basketball tournament.
contend.example_tw: 今年的籃球錦標賽有十支隊伍爭奪冠軍。

fuel.definition: a substance that generates energy or power after being burnt
fuel.example: Some people support the use of green fuels since they can power electric devices with environmentally friendly materials.
fuel.example_tw: 有些人支持使用綠色燃料，因為它們可以用環保材料為電子設備提供動力。

excessive.definition: more than what is considered necessary
excessive.example: An excessive amount of rain is forecast for the weekend, so people have been warned that there may be mudslides in the mountain region.
excessive.example_tw: 預計週末會有過量的降雨，因此已警告民眾山區可能會發生土石流。

melt.definition: to become liquid or to turn something into liquid by heating
melt.example: The heat from the pan caused the butter to melt.
melt.example_tw: 平底鍋的熱度使奶油融化了。

polar.definition: in relation to the North or South Pole
polar.example: Due to global warming, the polar regions are getting warmer, and ice caps are breaking down.
polar.example_tw: 由於全球暖化，極地地區變得越來越溫暖，冰帽也正在崩解。

overall.definition: referring to all the elements involved in something
overall.example: Nancy was satisfied with the overall care that her puppy had received. It looked so happy and clean when it came out of the dog hotel.
overall.example_tw: 南希對她的小狗受到的整體照顧感到滿意。牠從寵物旅館出來時看起來非常快樂和乾淨。

confirm.definition: to show that something is correct or true
confirm.example: The new evidence has confirmed the police's suspicion and proved that the man was guilty.
confirm.example_tw: 新證據證實了警方的懷疑，並證明該男子有罪。

incidence.definition: the rate at which something happens
incidence.example: A reduced incidence of hacking was reported after the bank tightened security around its customer information.
incidence.example_tw: 銀行加強客戶資訊安全後，駭客攻擊的發生率下降了。

sweep.definition: to move powerfully in a direction
sweep.example: The fierce hurricane swept into the city last night and destroyed several houses and cars.
sweep.example_tw: 昨晚猛烈的颶風席捲了這座城市，摧毀了數棟房屋和汽車。

extraordinary.example: The child had an extraordinary gift for singing. The audience was in tears after his performance.
extraordinary.example_tw: 這個孩子有著非凡的唱歌天賦。表演結束後，觀眾都感動落淚。

cease.definition: to bring something to an end or to end
cease.example: The soldiers were ordered to cease fire and withdraw from the battle.
cease.example_tw: 士兵們被命令停火並撤出戰鬥。

outlook.definition: the future of someone or something
outlook.example: The coach is optimistic about the outlook for the team in the upcoming game since the team has been preparing for it for a long time.
outlook.example_tw: 教練對球隊在即將到來的比賽中的前景感到樂觀，因為球隊已經為此準備了很長一段時間。

grim.definition: making someone feel unhappy or worried
grim.example: The company's financial situation looked grim as no investors could be found.
grim.example_tw: 由於找不到投資者，公司的財務狀況看起來令人沮喪。

joint.definition: involving two or more people or groups
joint.example: After getting married, the couple opened a joint bank account and deposited all of their money into it.
joint.example_tw: 結婚後，這對夫婦開了一個聯名銀行帳戶，並把所有的錢都存了進去。

curb.definition: to restrict or limit something that is typically considered bad
curb.example: As the situation worsened, the government took a series of emergency measures to curb the spread of the deadly virus.
curb.example_tw: 隨著局勢惡化，政府採取了一系列緊急措施來遏止致命病毒的傳播。

acknowledge.definition: to show that you are aware of the existence or truth of something
acknowledge.example: Rita acknowledged that her investment was a mistake after losing a considerable amount of money.
acknowledge.example_tw: 麗塔在損失了相當大的一筆錢後，承認她的投資是一個錯誤。

generation.definition: a group of people born at about the same time
generation.example: To protect future generations, we must take action to stop climate change from threatening the lives of our grandchildren and those born after.
generation.example_tw: 為了保護後代，我們必須採取行動阻止氣候變遷威脅我們子孫後代的生活。

march.definition: to walk through the streets to express ideas or protest about something
march.example: The crowd marched through the city to protest against the corrupt government.
march.example_tw: 人群在城市中遊行抗議腐敗的政府。

memorial.definition: a structure or item that is built in memory of someone or something
memorial.example: The city built a large statue as a memorial to the war heroes.
memorial.example_tw: 這座城市建了一座大型雕像作為戰爭英雄的紀念碑。

outline.definition: to describe the main ideas of something
outline.example: In the interview, Daniel outlined his college plans to the admission committee.
outline.example_tw: 在面試中，丹尼爾向招生委員會概述了他的大學計畫。

racism.definition: the belief that some races of people are better than others and the resulting unfair treatment of members of other races
racism.example: Both security guards were accused of racism after they automatically assumed the black man had stolen the jewelry.
racism.example_tw: 兩名保安在自動認定那名黑人偷了珠寶後被指控種族歧視。

decisive.definition: critical and powerful in influencing the outcome of a situation
decisive.example: A decisive factor in our victory was the goal Steve made in the last second of the game.
decisive.example_tw: 我們獲勝的決定性因素是史蒂夫在比賽最後一秒進的球。

civil.definition: having to do with the citizens of a country
civil.example: The civil war broke out mainly due to the conflicts between different races in the country.
civil.example_tw: 內戰爆發主要是因為該國不同種族之間的衝突。

grant.definition: to allow someone to get what he or she has requested, usually in a formal way
grant.example: The musician granted the director permission to use his music in the film.
grant.example_tw: 這位音樂家准予導演在電影中使用他的音樂。

flame.definition: the hot, bright burning gas from something on fire
flame.example: The two cars were in flames after they crashed into each other and exploded.
flame.example_tw: 兩輛車相撞爆炸後陷入火海。

wither.definition: (to cause) to dry up and die
wither.example: The severe drought withered all the plants in the fields.
wither.example_tw: 嚴重的乾旱使田裡所有的植物都枯萎了。

captivity.definition: the condition of being kept in a place and not allowed to leave
captivity.example: The animals in the circus are bred in captivity and suffer terrible physical abuse.
captivity.example_tw: 馬戲團的動物是在監禁中繁殖的，並遭受了可怕的身體虐待。

cripple.definition: to injure one's body so that he or she cannot walk or move normally
cripple.example: The car accident a few years ago left Jason crippled, and thus he has been in a wheelchair ever since.
cripple.example_tw: 幾年前的車禍使傑森殘廢，因此他從那時起就一直坐輪椅。

segregation.definition: the practice or policy of keeping people apart
segregation.example: The civil rights movement in the US sought to end racial segregation in public facilities.
segregation.example_tw: 美國的民權運動試圖結束公共設施中的種族隔離。

midst.example: In the midst of Claire's run, a wildcat jumped out of the bush and stopped her in her tracks.
midst.example_tw: 在克萊兒跑步的途中，一隻野貓從灌木叢中跳出來，讓她停下了腳步。

exile.definition: someone who has been banned from his or her homeland
exile.example: Unable to return home, the two political exiles have not seen their families for years.
exile.example_tw: 由於無法返家，這兩名政治流亡者已經多年沒有見到家人了。

shameful.definition: so wrong, bad, or ridiculous that one should feel ashamed
shameful.example: In Western societies, many people consider it shameful for a healthy adult to live off his or her parents.
shameful.example_tw: 在西方社會，許多人認為健康的成年人依賴父母生活是可恥的。

fatigue.definition: the condition of being extremely tired
fatigue.example: Larry's face was pale with fatigue because he had been working nonstop for three days.
fatigue.example_tw: 賴瑞因疲勞而臉色蒼白，因為他已經連續工作了三天。

mobility.definition: the state of being able to move freely from one location to another, or one job to another
mobility.example: When people are better-educated, they have greater upward career mobility.
mobility.example_tw: 當人們受過更好的教育時，他們有更大的職業向上流動性。

rob.definition: to take away something that someone has, wants, or deserves
rob.example: When Dan's parents went bankrupt, it robbed him of the chance to go to college.
rob.example_tw: 當丹的父母破產時，這剝奪了他上大學的機會。

dignity.definition: a sense of one's own worth or value as a person
dignity.example: Everyone should be treated with dignity regardless of his or her social status.
dignity.example_tw: 無論社會地位如何，每個人都應該被有尊嚴地對待。

oppression.definition: unfair and cruel treatment of people, especially by preventing them from having the same rights as others
oppression.example: After several decades of rule by a cruel leader, the people were finally freed from oppression when he died.
oppression.example_tw: 在一位殘暴的領導人統治了幾十年之後，當他去世時，人民終於從壓迫中解放出來。

vicious.example: Over one hundred people were injured or killed during the vicious terrorist attack.
vicious.example_tw: 在這次兇殘的恐怖襲擊中，有一百多人死傷。

symphony.definition: a long musical composition that involves different instruments
symphony.example: During the second movement of the symphony, the drums joined in to add a stronger beat to the music.
symphony.example_tw: 在交響樂的第二樂章中，鼓聲加入，為音樂增添了更強烈的節奏。

eloquence.definition: the gift or skill of being able to speak or express ideas very well
eloquence.example: The lawyer's eloquence moved the jury and won freedom for the client.
eloquence.example_tw: 律師的雄辯感動了陪審團，為委託人贏得了自由。

rhetorical.definition: dealing with the art of communication meant to persuade people
rhetorical.example: The use of rhetorical devices can make your speech both impressive and persuasive.
rhetorical.example_tw: 使用修辭技巧可以使你的演講令人印象深刻且具說服力。

conscience.definition: the part of your mind that tells you whether your behavior is morally right or wrong
conscience.example: We should listen to the voice of conscience when having to make a moral judgment.
conscience.example_tw: 在必須做出道德判斷時，我們應該傾聽良知的聲音。

warrior.definition: someone who fights in a war or battle
warrior.example: Amazon warriors bravely defended their homes when enemies attacked.
warrior.example_tw: 當敵人襲擊時，亞馬遜戰士勇敢地保衛她們的家園。
